title: Cyclaminus neapolitana
intro: Cyclamen neapolitanum, also known as Neapolitan cyclamen, is a perennial plant species belonging to the primrose family (Primulaceae). The plant is known for its heart-shaped leaves and vibrant, nodding flowers that come in shades of pink and white. The health benefits of Cyclamen neapolitanum include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, and it is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures.

The plant contains anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that help protect cells from damage and reduce inflammation. This can contribute to a lower risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer.
